name: Sole a La Catalane
recipeIngredient: 14 tbsp butter, unsalted, cut into pieces, plus more as needed
2 sole fillets, about 6 ounces each, split lengthwise
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 300°F.
2. Carefully remove the core from each tomato, leaving a small, narrow plug and keeping the tomato intact.
3. Blanch the tomatoes in boiling water for a few seconds, then drain under cold water and slip off their skins.
4. Cut a lid from the smooth bottom of each tomato (opposite the core) and set aside.
5. Using a melon scoop or spoon, carefully hollow out the pulp and seeds from the tomatoes, leaving a neat shell. Place the tomatoes and lids in a small buttered baking dish, season with salt and pepper, and set aside.
6. In a small skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Sauté the sliced onions until softened but not browned for 5 to 7 minutes.
7. Spoon the cooked onions into the hollowed tomatoes, dividing evenly.
8. On a work surface, gently flatten each sole fillet, tapping with the side of a wide knife blade. Roll up each fillet, starting from the small (tail) end and using the smooth, shiny skinned surface on the inside.
9. Lightly butter a heat-proof shallow baking dish in which the fillets will fit compactly. Scatter shallot into the dish, then arrange the rolled fish fillets over the shallot. Season with salt and pepper, pour wine over all, and cover with buttered parchment or waxed paper (buttered side down).
10. Place the pans over medium heat and bring almost to a boil.
11. Put the pans in the oven and bake until the fillets are just cooked through, 6 to 8 minutes. Do not overcook.
12. Remove the pans from the oven. Using a slotted spatula, lift each fillet, letting any liquid drain back into the pan, and place each fillet upright in a tomato.
13. Place the pan of fish-cooking liquid over high heat and boil until reduced to a few tablespoons of syrupy liquid.
14. Reduce the heat to very low. Whisk in the remaining 12 tablespoons of butter, 1 or 2 pieces at a time, adding more only when each addition becomes creamy and smooth.
15. Strain the sauce into a small bowl, stir in parsley and chives, and season with salt and pepper.
16. Place each stuffed tomato on a warm serving plate. Spoon some sauce over each fillet and around each tomato. Gently replace the lids and serve immediately.
